assertions.description: SpeechRecognition (or webkitSpeechRecognition) is exposed; the phrases attribute hangs off this interface.
SpeechRecognitionPhrase is a constructor; new SpeechRecognitionPhrase('foo', 2.0) succeeds.
The boost argument defaults to 1.0 when omitted.
SpeechRecognitionPhrase.phrase is readonly: writing to it is silently ignored (no setter).
A SpeechRecognition instance exposes a phrases attribute that supports Array-style push() for adding biasing phrases.
assertions.test: (()=>{const R=window.SpeechRecognition||window.webkitSpeechRecognition;if(!R)return false;try{const r=new R();return 'phrases' in r && typeof r.phrases.push==='function';}catch(_){return false;}})()

assertions.description: SpeechRecognition.prototype.start remains the entry point to actually run recognition; contextual biasing does not change this surface.
